reasoning: The acquisition of an independent nuclear weapon requires either a multi-year domestic development program or the direct transfer of a weapon from an existing nuclear-armed state. Neither scenario is plausible for a US ally in 2025. No current US ally without nuclear weapons (such as South Korea, Japan, Germany, or Saudi Arabia) has an active domestic weapons program capable of producing a bomb by the end of 2025. Furthermore, transferring independent control of a nuclear weapon violates the Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT), and no nuclear power (including the US) is expected to take such a radical step within this timeframe. Because Israel already possesses nuclear weapons, an official declaration by Israel would not fulfill the condition of not possessing weapons prior to March 10, 2025. Thus, the probability is at the minimal bound.
